2603.17769.title: Modeling Overlapped Speech with Shuffles
2603.17769.paper_summary: We propose to model parallel streams of data, such as overlapped speech, using shuffles. Specifically, this paper shows how the shuffle product and partial order finite-state automata (FSAs) can be used for alignment and speaker-attributed transcription of overlapped speech. We train using the total score on these FSAs as a loss function, marginalizing over all possible serializations of overlapping sequences at subword, word, and phrase levels. To reduce graph size, we impose temporal constraints by constructing partial order FSAs. We address speaker attribution by modeling (token, speaker) tuples directly. Viterbi alignment through the shuffle product FSA directly enables one-pass alignment. We evaluate performance on synthetic LibriSpeech overlaps. To our knowledge, this is the first algorithm that enables single-pass alignment of multi-talker recordings. All algorithms are implemented using k2 / Icefall.
2603.17769.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出使用洗牌來建模平行數據流，例如重疊的語音。具體而言，本文展示了如何使用洗牌乘積和部分有序有限狀態自動機（FSA）來對重疊語音進行對齊和說話者歸屬轉錄。我們使用這些 FSA 的總分作為損失函數，並在子詞、單詞和短語層面上對所有可能的重疊序列進行邊際化。為了減少圖形大小，我們通過構建部分有序 FSA 來施加時間約束。我們通過直接建模（標記、說話者）元組來解決說話者歸屬問題。通過洗牌乘積 FSA 的 Viterbi 對齊直接實現了一次性對齊。我們在合成的 LibriSpeech 重疊上評估性能。據我們所知，這是第一個能夠實現多講者錄音的一次性對齊的算法。所有算法均使用 k2 / Icefall 實現。
2603.17769.author: Matthew Wiesner et.al.
2603.17769.authors: Matthew Wiesner, Samuele Cornell, Alexander Polok, Lucas Ondel Yang, Lukáš Burget, Sanjeev Khudanpur
